__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics.

description: mdk saturation view: worker in-flight depth (mdk.jobs.in_flight) + Postgres pool saturation gauges (mdk.db.pool.* -- ADR 034 D3). Prescriptive layer: triage flow + per-chart notes + drill-down links. These are the early-warning signals under KEDA autoscale: in-flight rising without throughput catching up = workers stuck; in_use approaching max + sustained non-zero waiting = pool exhaustion. mdk doctor (ADR 034 D1) does the static math. Pool gauges are flat/zero on the local SQLite backend.

options.content:
## mdk - queue & pool saturation

**What this shows.** The two saturation surfaces that precede outages under autoscale: worker in-flight depth (`mdk.jobs.in_flight`) and per-pod Postgres pool stats (`mdk.db.pool.size/idle/in_use/waiting/max`).

**Normal range.**

- In-flight rises with traffic, falls with throughput; steady-state ~ `arrival_rate * avg_duration`.
- `in_use / max` < 0.7 per pod (orange at 0.7, red at 0.9 - already wired on the per-pod saturation panel).
- `waiting` = 0 (any sustained non-zero is the pool bottleneck).
- `pool_size`, `pool_in_use`, `pool_max` all flat/zero on SQLite backend.

**No SLO alert rules** target these gauges directly today; they precede `*-high-error-rate` (503s from connection exhaustion) and `*-high-latency-p95` (workers stuck on slow tools).

**Triage flow.**

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Latency or error SLO firing] --> B{In-flight rising, throughput flat?}
  B -- yes --> B1[Workers stuck on slow tool/LLM - Jaeger minDuration=10s]
  B -- no  --> C{Pool in_use / max > 0.9?}
  C -- yes --> C1{Waiting > 0?}
  C1 -- yes --> C2[Pool ceiling too tight - raise pool_max OR add pods]
  C1 -- no  --> C3[Hot pod; KEDA scale-out should relieve - confirm replicas]
  C -- no  --> D{Waiting > 0 sustained?}
  D -- yes --> D1[Acquire queue building - check pool_max config in storage init]
  D -- no  --> E[Saturation elsewhere; check golden-signals dashboard]
  C2 --> F[Mind the Postgres max_connections ceiling: N_pods * pool_max]
  F --> G[Run mdk doctor capacity check ADR 034 D1]
```

List fallback:
1. In-flight rising flat throughput -> workers stuck.
2. `in_use/max > 0.9` + waiting > 0 -> raise pool_max OR pods (but mind Postgres `max_connections`).
3. Waiting > 0 alone -> acquire queue building.
4. Run `mdk doctor` for static capacity check.

**Cross-surface.** KQL equivalent in `infra/azure-monitor/workbooks/platform.workbook.json` (pool stats + queue depth + revisions).

panels.description: Worker in-flight job count (claimed, not yet terminal) by tenant. Up-down counter -- no _total suffix; bracketed inc/dec in src/movate/runtime/worker.py.
panels.title: In-flight by tenant

options.content:
**What this measures.** Per-tenant count of jobs currently claimed by workers (proxy for queue/worker saturation). Source: `mdk.jobs.in_flight`.

**Normal.** Rises and falls with traffic. Steady-state ~ `arrival_rate * avg_duration`. No alert rule today (true queue depth deferred per ADR 031).

**If you see X.**

- `rising while throughput flat (golden-signals dashboard)` -> workers stuck. Open Jaeger with `minDuration=10s`; the long traces will show a stuck `skill.<name>` or LLM call.
- `stuck non-zero with zero throughput` -> consumer dead. Check `mdk worker` pod health + KEDA scale (use platform workbook revisions section), watch for `*-availability-no-traffic`.
- `monotonically rising over hours` -> leaking in-flight counter (inc without dec); verify `dec_in_flight` runs on every terminal path in `src/movate/runtime/worker.py`.

**ADR refs.** ADR 019 (queue + W3C context) / ADR 031. **Alert rules.** None today (related: `*-availability-no-traffic`).

panels.description: Per-pod pool composition: open (size), idle, in-use (size - idle). Observable gauges sampled at OTel collection time.
panels.title: Pool composition (fleet)

panels.description: Pool saturation = in_use / max. Approaches 1.0 -> connection exhaustion imminent on this pod.
panels.title: Per-pod pool saturation (in_use / max; orange 0.7, red 0.9)

options.content:
**What this measures.** Fleet pool composition (open/idle/in_use across pods) and per-pod saturation ratio (`in_use / max`).

**Normal.** `in_use/max` < 0.7 (green); 0.7-0.9 (orange watch); > 0.9 (red - exhaustion imminent on that pod).

**If you see X.**

- `one pod red, others green` -> hot pod; KEDA scale-out should relieve. Confirm replica count in Container Apps revision view.
- `multiple pods red` -> the ceiling is too tight fleet-wide. Decide: raise per-pod `pool_max` OR add pods. **Critical:** `N_pods * pool_max` must stay under Azure Postgres `max_connections` minus headroom. Run `mdk doctor` (ADR 034 D1) before applying either lever.
- `in_use rising with throughput flat` -> connection leak from a recently-added call site. Bisect against deploys.
- `flat at 0` -> SQLite backend (no pool); or asyncpg meter callback failing - check stderr for `register_pool_metrics` errors.

**ADR refs.** ADR 034 (D1 static doctor / D3 live gauges). **Alert rule.** None today; saturation precedes `*-high-error-rate` once the pool exhausts.

panels.description: Callers blocked waiting on the acquire queue (mdk.db.pool.waiting). Sustained non-zero = the pool ceiling is too tight for the offered load; revisit pool_max or pod count.
panels.title: Pool acquire waiters (early-warning; red line = any waiter)

options.content:
**What this measures.** Total callers blocked on the pool acquire queue (`mdk.db.pool.waiting`). Red threshold = **any non-zero** (this should be 0 in a healthy steady state).

**Normal.** Zero. Brief blips during scale events are tolerable, sustained non-zero is not.

**If you see X.**

- `sustained > 0` -> pool ceiling too tight. Raise `pool_max` OR pod count - run `mdk doctor` (ADR 034 D1) first so you don't trip the Postgres `max_connections` ceiling.
- `spiky with KEDA scale events` -> new pods initializing the pool while existing ones absorb load; usually self-clears. If it doesn't, the warmup pool is too small.
- `waiting climbing while in_use flat` -> deadlock-like behavior in storage; pull `agent.execute` traces with long `db.execute` children in Jaeger.

**ADR refs.** ADR 034. **Alert rule.** None today; precedes `*-high-error-rate` once acquires time out and propagate as 503s.

title: mdk - queue & pool saturation
